attendance.general_requirement: Minimum 75% attendance is mandatory in most Indian colleges for appearing in semester/annual exams.
attendance.strict_colleges: Some colleges like IITs, NITs, and medical colleges require 85% attendance.
attendance.medical_condonation: Students with attendance between 65-75% may apply for medical/other condonation with valid documents.
attendance.detention: Students with less than 65% attendance may be detained from appearing in exams.
attendance.how_to_check: Attendance is usually available on the college's student portal, ERP system, or notified by respective department/class coordinator.
attendance.leave_application: Submit leave applications to the class advisor or HoD within 3 days of returning from leave with supporting documents.

pattern.semester_system: Two semesters per year: Odd (July-November) and Even (December-April/May). Each semester has Internal Assessments (IA) + End Semester Exams (ESE).
pattern.annual_system: Annual exams held at the end of the academic year (common in Arts/Science colleges).
pattern.cbcs: Choice Based Credit System (CBCS): Students earn credits for each course; minimum credits required to graduate.
pattern.semester_credit_hours: Most UG programs require 160-180 total credits; PG programs 60-80 credits.

components: Class tests / Cycle tests (2-3 per semester)
internal_assessment.minimum_pass: Students must score minimum marks in both internal and external to pass.

10_point_cgpa.used_by: Most technical universities (affiliated with VTU, Anna University, JNTU, RGPV, etc.) and central universities
10_point_cgpa.cgpa_formula: CGPA = Sum(Grade Points × Credit Hours) / Total Credit Hours

percentage_system.used_by: Traditional universities, Arts/Science/Commerce colleges, Medical colleges
percentage_system.pass_mark: Minimum 35-40% in each subject to pass; 50% for distinction

examination.supplementary_exams: Students who fail in one or more subjects can appear for supplementary/re-examination held after the main exam results. Usually conducted 1-2 times a year.
examination.re_evaluation: Students can apply for re-evaluation/re-totalling of answer scripts on payment of fees within the specified time after result declaration.
examination.result_declaration: Results are declared on the official university website or student portal, typically 30-45 days after exams end.

odd_semester.start: Late July / First week of August

course_registration.process: Students register for courses at the beginning of each semester through online portals (ERP/SLMS/etc.)
course_registration.credit_load: Minimum 14 credits and maximum 28 credits per semester typically.
course_registration.electives: Core courses are mandatory; students choose electives from available options based on interests and career goals.
course_registration.late_registration: Late registration fees apply after the deadline. Contact academic office for help.

backlog_policy.definition: A 'backlog' or 'arrear' means a failed subject that needs to be cleared.
backlog_policy.clearance: Backlogs must be cleared within the maximum time limit (usually program duration + 2-3 years).
backlog_policy.impact_on_placement: Many companies require students to be 'arrear-free' at the time of placement. Check company-specific criteria.
backlog_policy.lateral_entry: Diploma holders can join B.Tech in 2nd year (lateral entry), giving credit for completed diploma courses.

engineering.national_level: JEE Main (for NITs, IIITs, CFTIs) and JEE Advanced (for IITs). Conducted by NTA and IIT respectively.
engineering.state_level: Each state has its own entrance: TNEA (Tamil Nadu), MHT-CET (Maharashtra), KCET (Karnataka), AP EAMCET, TS EAMCET, WBJEE (West Bengal), OJEE (Odisha), etc.
engineering.management_quota: 15-20% seats in private colleges filled through management quota (direct admission based on merit + donation).
engineering.nri_quota: Some private colleges reserve seats for NRI students.
engineering.counselling: State-level counselling conducted by JoSAA/CSAB (national) or state counselling boards for seat allotment.

management_mba.process: Written test → Group Discussion (GD) → Personal Interview (PI) → Final merit list

medical.exam: NEET-UG (for MBBS, BDS, BAMS, BHMS, BVSc) — conducted by NTA
medical.counselling: MCC conducts national counselling; state medical councils conduct state counselling.

law.national: CLAT (Common Law Admission Test) for NLUs
law.other: AILET (NLU Delhi), LSAT India, state law entrance exams

arts_science_commerce.process: Most colleges admit based on Class 12 merit (percentage). Some have entrance exams.
arts_science_commerce.du_example: Delhi University uses CUET (Common University Entrance Test) for UG admissions.
arts_science_commerce.cuet: CUET (Common University Entrance Test) by NTA increasingly used by central universities for UG admissions.

central_government.name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) Scholarships
central_government.eligibility: SC/ST/OBC/Minority/EWS students with income below specified limit

central_government.name: Central Sector Scheme (CSS)
central_government.eligibility: Top 20 percentile in Class 12 board, family income below Rs. 8 lakh/year

central_government.name: AICTE Pragati & Saksham Scholarships
central_government.eligibility: Pragati: Girl students in AICTE-approved institutions; Saksham: Differently-abled students

central_government.name: PM Yasasvi Scholarship
central_government.eligibility: OBC, EBC, and DNT students with family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh/year

scholarships.state_government: Each state has its own scholarship schemes for domicile students. Check state social welfare department websites.
scholarships.institution_scholarships: Merit-based scholarships, sports scholarships, cultural achievement scholarships offered by individual colleges.
scholarships.iit_jee_fee_waiver: Students with family income below Rs. 1 lakh/year get full fee waiver at IITs/NITs. Below Rs. 5 lakh get partial waiver.

bonafide_certificate.purpose: Proof that you are a bonafide student of the institution. Required for bank loans, internship applications, railway concession, etc.
bonafide_certificate.how_to_get: Apply at the college office / student affairs office with your ID card. Usually issued within 2-3 working days.

migration_certificate.purpose: Required when a student migrates from one university to another.
migration_certificate.how_to_get: Apply at the university registrar's office after completing or leaving the program. Submit No Objection Certificate from current institution.

character_certificate.purpose: Certifies good character/conduct during study. Needed for employment, higher education abroad.
character_certificate.how_to_get: Apply to the Principal/Dean with application form. Takes 3-5 days.

transcript.purpose: Official record of academic performance. Required for foreign university applications.
transcript.how_to_get: Apply at the controller of examinations office or registrar's office. Fees apply. Official transcripts sent directly to institutions.

tc_leaving_certificate.purpose: Transfer Certificate (TC) is issued when a student leaves the institution permanently.
tc_leaving_certificate.process: Clear all dues (fees, library, hostel). Apply at principal's office with a request letter. Get No Dues certificate from all departments.

government_colleges.tuition_fee: Rs. 5,000 – Rs. 30,000 per year for most government engineering/arts colleges
government_colleges.other_fees: Development fee, exam fee, library fee, sports fee, lab fee

private_colleges.tuition_fee: Rs. 50,000 – Rs. 3,00,000+ per year depending on college and program
private_colleges.iim_mba: Rs. 20-25 lakh for full 2-year MBA program

modes: Online portal (net banking, UPI, credit/debit card)
DD (Demand Draft) in favour of institution
fee_payment.late_fine: Late fee payment attracts penalty of Rs. 5-50 per day depending on institution.
fee_payment.installments: Some colleges allow fee payment in 2-3 installments per semester. Check with accounts section.
